tenant.company_legal_name: University of Oldbury Students' Guild Ltd

activation_key.notes: Demo client for JWEBLY system pilot. First education sector deployment.

current_tools.name: Microsoft 365
current_tools.notes: Good for email and docs, but poor for workflow automation. No cross-department visibility.

current_tools.name: Trello
current_tools.notes: Team rarely updates boards, becomes stale quickly. Officers abandon it mid-term.

current_tools.name: WhatsApp Groups
current_tools.notes: Chaotic, messages get lost, no accountability. 47 different groups, context switches constantly.

current_tools.name: Google Forms
current_tools.notes: Basic but limited analysis. Data trapped in spreadsheets, no integration.

current_tools.name: Custom WordPress
current_tools.notes: Functional but outdated, hard to maintain. No one remembers how to update plugins.

current_tools.name: QuickBooks
current_tools.notes: Works well but not integrated with society budgets. Manual reconciliation takes hours.

current_tools.name: Canva
current_tools.notes: Marketing team loves it, but brand assets scattered. No asset management.

current_tools.name: MailChimp
current_tools.notes: Segments outdated, lists not synced with membership. Low open rates.

current_tools.name: Paper Diary
current_tools.notes: Room bookings in physical diary. Double bookings happen weekly.

pain_points.description: Student societies submit event proposals via email, leading to lost requests and duplicate submissions
pain_points.impact: 20+ hours/week spent on manual tracking

pain_points.description: Society budget requests require 4-5 email exchanges before approval, with no visibility into queue position
pain_points.impact: Societies miss event windows while waiting for approval

pain_points.description: Equipment loan tracking is done on paper - items go missing, no one knows what's available
pain_points.impact: £3,000+ equipment replaced annually due to losses

pain_points.description: BUCS fixture coordination relies on personal memory - conflicts with academic schedules not caught early
pain_points.impact: Teams forfeit matches due to scheduling conflicts

pain_points.description: Course rep feedback forms submitted but never systematically analysed - patterns invisible
pain_points.impact: Same issues raised year after year with no resolution tracking

pain_points.description: Academic appeal support requests handled in email - no case tracking, no precedent lookup
pain_points.impact: Inconsistent advice given, students fall through gaps

pain_points.description: Student feedback on teaching quality is collected but not correlated with course changes
pain_points.impact: Cannot demonstrate student voice leading to improvements

pain_points.description: Advice centre cases tracked in Excel with no workflow automation or follow-up reminders
pain_points.impact: Students wait 3-5 days for initial response, vulnerable cases missed

pain_points.description: Hardship fund applications paper-based, processing takes 2-3 weeks when students need urgent help
pain_points.impact: Students in crisis wait too long, some drop out

pain_points.description: Safeguarding concerns logged but no pattern recognition - can't see repeat issues
pain_points.impact: Early warning signs missed, reactive not proactive

pain_points.description: Mental health first aider deployments tracked in personal calendars, no oversight
pain_points.impact: Burnout risk for first aiders, coverage gaps

pain_points.description: Social media content calendar in Trello, video assets in Canva, copy in Google Docs - nothing linked
pain_points.impact: 2-3 hours daily searching for assets and context

pain_points.description: Societies request promotional support via DMs, email, WhatsApp - requests lost constantly
pain_points.impact: Events poorly promoted, societies frustrated

pain_points.description: Newsletter audience segments outdated - sending irrelevant content to students
pain_points.impact: 15% open rate, high unsubscribes

pain_points.description: Video archive unorganised - hours spent recreating content that exists somewhere
pain_points.impact: Duplicated effort, inconsistent quality

pain_points.description: 120+ society accounts reconciled manually in QuickBooks - one person's full-time job
pain_points.impact: Delayed financial reporting, audit risk

pain_points.description: Expense claims processed via paper forms, receipts get lost, reimbursements take weeks
pain_points.impact: Staff frustration, out-of-pocket costs

pain_points.description: Charity Commission reporting requires manual data gathering from 6+ sources
pain_points.impact: 3-week annual process, compliance risk

pain_points.description: Trustee meeting papers prepared manually, previous decisions not easily searchable
pain_points.impact: Trustees make decisions without full historical context

pain_points.description: Room bookings in paper diary - double bookings happen weekly, no visibility for planners
pain_points.impact: Events disrupted, staff time wasted resolving conflicts

pain_points.description: Event risk assessments filed in folders, not linked to bookings - H&S gaps
pain_points.impact: Compliance risk, potential liability

pain_points.description: Supplier contracts stored in filing cabinet, renewal dates missed
pain_points.impact: Unfavourable auto-renewals, missed negotiation opportunities

pain_points.description: Building maintenance requests via email, no prioritisation or tracking
pain_points.impact: Issues persist for months, reactive maintenance

pain_points.description: AGM motions submitted by email, tracking and communication ad hoc
pain_points.impact: Low student engagement, questions about legitimacy

pain_points.description: Policy decisions not systematically recorded - precedent unclear
pain_points.impact: Inconsistent decisions, re-debating settled issues

pain_points.description: Election candidate management done in spreadsheets, no workflow
pain_points.impact: Candidate experience poor, complaints about fairness

pain_points.description: Sabbatical officer handover is chaotic - institutional knowledge lost every year
pain_points.impact: 2-3 month productivity loss each summer

pain_points.description: Each new officer reinvents processes - no record of what worked before
pain_points.impact: Repeated failures, wasted innovation

pain_points.description: Staff knowledge concentrated in 2-3 long-serving people - bus factor of 1
pain_points.impact: Organisation paralysed if key staff sick or leave

pain_points.description: Previous manifesto commitments and outcomes not tracked - can't learn from past
pain_points.impact: Same promises made, same mistakes repeated

pain_points.description: No centralised view of all guild activities - information scattered across email, Trello, and WhatsApp
pain_points.impact: Decisions made with incomplete information

pain_points.description: Cannot track student engagement across different guild services
pain_points.impact: Funding applications lack evidence of impact

bottlenecks.impact: Events cancelled due to late approvals

bottlenecks.current_lead_time: No system - first come first served

bottlenecks.impact: Patterns not surfaced until too late

bottlenecks.desired_lead_time: Same day triage, urgent cases immediate

bottlenecks.impact: Staff and students out of pocket
Confusion about what's being voted on

primary_goals.goal: Automate society event approval workflow with intelligent routing and auto-approval for standard requests
primary_goals.success_criteria: 80% of standard events auto-approved within 24 hours

primary_goals.goal: Create institutional memory system that captures decision context, outcomes, and lessons learned
primary_goals.success_criteria: New officers productive within 2 weeks, can query past decisions

primary_goals.goal: Implement unified welfare case management with automated triage and pattern detection
primary_goals.success_criteria: Same-day triage for all cases, urgent cases flagged automatically

primary_goals.goal: Centralise all guild operations in single dashboard with role-based views
primary_goals.success_criteria: 100% team adoption, eliminate WhatsApp for work communication

primary_goals.goal: Automate society budget tracking with real-time visibility and self-service requests
primary_goals.success_criteria: Societies can track own budgets, requests processed in 1 week

primary_goals.goal: Implement digital room booking system with conflict prevention and equipment integration
primary_goals.success_criteria: Zero double bookings, equipment availability visible at booking

primary_goals.goal: Build course rep feedback analytics with theme detection and trend tracking
primary_goals.success_criteria: Real-time dashboard of student concerns by department/course

primary_goals.goal: Create content request workflow with asset management and approval tracking
primary_goals.success_criteria: All promotional requests processed in 3 days, asset library searchable

primary_goals.goal: Implement transparent audit trail for all financial transactions and decisions
primary_goals.success_criteria: Audit prep reduced to 1 week, real-time compliance dashboard

primary_goals.goal: Build student engagement analytics for funding evidence and impact reporting
primary_goals.success_criteria: Automated impact reports for grant applications

success_metrics.target: Same day for all, urgent immediate
Any past decision findable in < 30 seconds
success_metrics.current_baseline: Not possible - knowledge in people's heads

societies_activities.description: Managing 120+ student societies and 15 sports clubs

welfare_support.description: Student welfare, advice and support services

marketing_comms.description: Communications, social media, and content creation

handover_requirements.description: Critical institutional memory needs for annual officer transition

current_state.documentation: Ad hoc Word documents, often not completed
current_state.system_access: Credentials on sticky notes, accounts not properly transitioned

desired_state.documentation: Living documents automatically updated with decision context
desired_state.knowledge_transfer: Queryable institutional memory - ask questions, get contextual answers
desired_state.system_access: Automated credential rotation and access provisioning

key_knowledge_to_capture: Why decisions were made, not just what was decided
What was tried and didn't work (negative knowledge)
Precedents and how similar situations were handled
What each manifesto commitment actually meant in practice
handover_documents_needed: What I wish I'd known when I started

integration_requirements.purpose: Calendar sync, email integration, SSO, document access
integration_requirements.notes: University provides M365 licenses. SSO would reduce password fatigue.

integration_requirements.purpose: Two-way budget sync, invoice automation, expense tracking
integration_requirements.notes: Need read/write access. Must sync society sub-accounts.

integration_requirements.purpose: Verify student status for membership, course/department data
integration_requirements.notes: University IT approval required - process started. GDPR considerations.

integration_requirements.purpose: Society payment tracking, event ticketing reconciliation
integration_requirements.notes: Already have Stripe Connect setup for society payments.

integration_requirements.purpose: Asset management integration, brand template sync
integration_requirements.notes: Marketing team already proficient, just need asset organisation.

data_migration_needs.notes: Database is 15 years old, needs significant cleanup. Schema undocumented.

data_migration_needs.data_type: Historical event records, project notes, decisions
data_migration_needs.notes: Extract insights for knowledge base. Many boards abandoned.

data_migration_needs.data_type: Policy documents, procedures, templates, meeting minutes
data_migration_needs.notes: Need full text search capability. Current folder structure chaotic.
For pattern recognition and budget forecasting.
Critical institutional context in email. Selective import.

compliance_requirements.notes: Student data is personal data under GDPR. Special category data in welfare.
Registered charity - annual returns required, public benefit demonstration.
Must comply with university data governance. Joint controller scenarios.
Working with vulnerable students - DBS checks for relevant roles.
Must meet NUS democratic standards for affiliation.

security_requirements.requirement: Audit logging for sensitive data access
Encryption at rest and in transit
Automated credential rotation on role changes

stakeholder_map.name: Sarah Thompson
stakeholder_map.notes: Term ends July 2026. Wants legacy project.

stakeholder_map.name: David Chen
stakeholder_map.engagement: Cautious supporter - burned by past failures
stakeholder_map.notes: 15 years institutional knowledge. Key to operational buy-in.

stakeholder_map.name: Rachel Williams
stakeholder_map.notes: 12 years experience. Owns budget and compliance.

stakeholder_map.name: Laura Martinez
stakeholder_map.notes: Will be primary daily user for societies workflow.

discovery_audit.agency_notes:
University of Oldbury Students' Guild is a well-established student union serving approximately 12,000 students. They have 5 elected sabbatical officers (full-time paid positions for students) who change annually, plus 18 permanent staff members. The organisation has significant challenges with knowledge transfer due to the annual turnover of leadership.

The Guild manages approximately 120 student societies and 15 sports clubs, each with their own budgets, events, and leadership. Event coordination is their biggest operational headache, but the deeper issue is institutional memory.

THE KILLER INSIGHT: Every July, 5 new officers arrive knowing nothing. They spend 2-3 months just learning how things work. By the time they're effective, they're planning their exit. The permanent staff (David, Rachel) carry everything, but they're stretched thin and becoming single points of failure.

JWEBLY here isn't just about workflows - it's about building an 'organisational brain' that remembers what the people forget. Every decision, every context, every 'why we do it this way' captured and queryable. When a new officer asks 'has anyone tried X before?' the system should know.

Sarah Thompson (Guild President) is highly motivated and technically literate. She's in her final year and wants to leave a 'lasting legacy system' before her term ends in July 2026. This creates both urgency and risk - we need to ensure the system outlasts her tenure.

The permanent staff, particularly David Chen (Operations Manager) and Rachel Williams (Finance Manager), are the institutional memory. They are supportive but have been burned by past 'digital transformation' attempts that were abandoned when the championing officer left.

RECOMMENDED APPROACH:
1. Quick win: Society event approval workflow (visible improvement, high frequency)
2. Foundation: Room booking system (eliminates daily pain)
3. Strategic: Knowledge base with context capture (the long-term value)
4. Critical path: Handover system ready before July 2026

Potential case study opportunity if successful - education sector is underserved by operational intelligence tools. Student unions are a perfect test case for 'high turnover leadership' problem that affects many organisations.

RISKS:
- Sarah leaves before system embedded - mitigation: David/Rachel as co-owners
- Scope creep from officer enthusiasm - mitigation: phased rollout
- University IT blocking integrations - mitigation: work standalone first
- Staff resistance to 'another system' - mitigation: show immediate value

prohibited_responses: Never give legal advice on employment or academic matters
Never process welfare referrals without human review
Never approve budget changes above £500 without human approval
Never share individual student data across departments
Never make safeguarding decisions - always escalate to designated officer
institutional_memory_prompts: Have we tried this approach before?
What happened last time we did X?
Who handled this type of situation previously?
What was the context behind this decision?
What did the previous officer learn about this?

societies_activities.scenario: Drama Society requests £500 additional budget for spring production 3 weeks before event
societies_activities.typical_handling: 4-5 email exchanges, Activities Manager to Finance, waiting for committee approval
societies_activities.desired_handling: Self-service request, auto-routing based on amount, approval within 48 hours

societies_activities.scenario: Two societies both request main hall for same Friday evening
societies_activities.typical_handling: First person to check paper diary wins, second society only finds out when they call
societies_activities.desired_handling: Real-time availability, waitlist functionality, auto-notification of alternatives

welfare_support.scenario: Student discloses financial hardship during drop-in, needs emergency fund
welfare_support.typical_handling: Paper form, 2-3 week processing, student anxiety increases
welfare_support.desired_handling: Digital submission, same-day triage, 48-hour emergency processing track

welfare_support.scenario: Course rep reports multiple students struggling in same module
welfare_support.typical_handling: Email to VP Education, no systematic tracking of pattern
welfare_support.desired_handling: Auto-aggregation of feedback, pattern flagging, trend dashboard

handover.scenario: New VP Activities asks 'Did we ever consider a winter ball?'
handover.typical_handling: Ask David (if he remembers), search old emails (if they're accessible)
handover.desired_handling: Query knowledge base: 'winter ball attempts' - get 2019 and 2022 attempts with outcomes and lessons

handover.scenario: New officer making decision similar to one made 2 years ago
handover.typical_handling: Reinvent the wheel, possibly make same mistake
handover.desired_handling: System surfaces 'Similar decision made in 2024: [context] - outcome was [X]'
